title: Why Are Energy Policies Acceptable and Effective?
authors: Linda Steg, Lieke Dreijerink, Wokje Abrahamse

excluded_policy_mentions.exact_term_used_in_paper: pricing policies aimed to reduce CO2 emissions
excluded_policy_mentions.reason_it_is_not_a_carbon_or_fuel_tax: Generic energy-price policies; no explicit carbon tax, fuel tax, gasoline tax, or fossil-fuel excise is analyzed.

inclusion_reason: Directly studies acceptability and perceived effectiveness of energy pricing policies, but the instruments are not explicit carbon or fuel taxes.
exclusion_reason: Focuses on generic pricing policies for household energy use and appliances, not a clearly identified carbon/fuel tax instrument.
